response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems software engineer with a solid ETH Zürich CS background and 15+ years of experience, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His work is entirely in web, mobile, and backend software, with the closest hardware-adjacent experience being low-latency network services and embedded systems tooling. Recent AI-agent infrastructure work is software tooling, not ML workload or accelerator optimization.
strengths: Strong CS foundation (ETH Zürich) with 15+ years of diverse technical experience across multiple domains and languages
Demonstrated performance engineering in high-throughput, low-latency systems (millions of HTTP req/sec at Sociomantic using async I/O and caching)
Broad systems breadth including embedded, mobile, backend, and some AI-agent infrastructure work
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or AI accelerator experience — the core of this role
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD workloads
Entire career is software-only (web, mobile, full-stack); no hardware-software co-design or microarchitecture-level work of any kind

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware architecture experience
No DL workload or AI accelerator expertise
Performance engineering in low-latency software systems
